criterion_scores.name: correct-reconciled-total
criterion_scores.judge_rationale: Reports a wrong total ($312,175) that matches neither the CRM sum (375,740) nor the finance sum (370,640). The candidate merely sums one file incorrectly with no reconciliation of the duplicate (+8,400) or Everest (−13,500) — matches score 1 anchor: 'Reports a wrong total, or just sums one file ... with no reconciliation of the difference.'

criterion_scores.name: identifies-real-vs-cosmetic
criterion_scores.judge_rationale: Misses two of the three genuine issues: the Bluecrest duplicate is dismissed as 'not an error,' and the Halcyon month mismatch is absent entirely. Only Everest is caught. Matches score 1 anchor: 'misses that the duplicate/Everest/Halcyon rows are the substantive issues.'

criterion_scores.name: finds-duplicate
criterion_scores.judge_rationale: Notes a duplicate for INV-1012 but explicitly calls it 'not an error' and does not identify it as a duplicated row to remove or quantify its +8,400 effect. Matches score 1 anchor: 'Does not notice the duplicate; counts Bluecrest twice.'

criterion_scores.name: flags-ambiguity-not-guesses
criterion_scores.judge_rationale: Silently 'corrects' Everest to $15,000 ('should be $15,000') with no assumption stated; Halcyon month disagreement is not mentioned at all. Matches score 1 anchor: 'Silently "corrects" Everest to one value as if certain ... treats the Halcyon month as settled ... no assumptions stated.'

criterion_scores.name: discrepancy-list-quality
criterion_scores.judge_rationale: Line-by-line list marks every row 'Match' except Everest; the duplicate and Halcyon rows are omitted as genuine discrepancies with causes. Cosmetic noise is not summarized. Matches score 1 anchor: 'differences are asserted vaguely or not enumerated.'
